title: vicia varia subsp. villosa
intro: Vicia varia subsp. villosa, also known as hairy vetch or vetchling, is a perennial plant in the pea family that is native to Europe and Western Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. Vicia varia subsp. villosa is believed to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as being used as a treatment for respiratory issues and skin conditions. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for medicinal use, although its effectiveness and safety have not been extensively scientifically studied.
